projects.raw_text:
Reducing Glitch Power in Digital Designs

Jun 2021 – Dec 2021


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

The increased number of glitches in today's ICs led to increasing in power consumption. This is more prominent in circuits such as multipliers & adders. Almost 70% of dynamic power is due to glitches.
As low power designs are quite popular nowadays, but at particular threshold voltage, glitches tend to increase irrespective of the structure if we lower the supply voltage.
So various techniques have been developed at various stages of the VLSI design flow, such as at architectural level, Netlist level, PNR level, etc., to handle and reduce the power due to glitches.

RTL to GDS Flow

Feb 2021 – May 2021


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Implemented full RTL to GDS flow for given specification at 90nm technology. The steps included Logical Synthesis (Genus Tool), Formal Equivalence (Conformal low power GXL tool), Static Timing Analysis (Tempus tool) & Design for Test. For Physical Design flow(Encounter tool), Floorplanning, Clock Tree Synthesis, Placement, Routing, ECO, and finally, GDSII was written off after timing closure using Cadence Encounter.

Skills: Design Rule Checking (DRC), Timing Closure, +1 skill

Other contributors

View all contributors
Design of Low Leakage 6T SRAM Cell

Feb 2021 – Apr 2021


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

6T SRAM Cell was designed for given low leakage specification over different temperature ranges.The Various figures of merits such as SNM, Cell Current, Write time, Write margin were calculated & having 6 sigma qualification. Various trade-offs between the different figures of merits were also observed.

Skills: Design Rule Checking (DRC)

Layered ACO OFDM

Feb 2021 – Apr 2021


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

The BER performance has been analysed in the face of interlayer interference and clipping distortion. The higher layers tend to have worse BER performance due to interference imposed by lower layers, while the first layer has almost same performance as ACO-OFDM. The LACO-OFDM PAPR is significantly influenced by its first layer. Adding more layers in a TD signal results in lower PAPR. To improve PAPR a tone injection method was proposed for LACO OFDM.

 Half Latch based Level shifter & Wilson Current Mirror based Level shifter

Oct 2020 – Nov 2020


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Designed & simulated Level shifter based on Half latch & Wilson current mirror using Cadence Virtuoso.
The circuit was designed at 90nm gpdk technology. The delay and power dissipation was measured for both of the circuits. 

Design & Layout of OAI221 complex gate using Domino Logic at 65nm technology.

Oct 2020 – Nov 2020


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Designed a OAI221 complex gate based on Domino Logic. The Layout was made & verified for DRC, LVS checks. The design was made using cadence virtuoso and eldo was used for the simulation results.
Pre-post analysis was done and the difference between the two was minimal.

Skills: Design Rule Checking (DRC)

Other contributors



View all contributors
Review paper on Semiconductor Lasers

Oct 2020 – Nov 2020


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

The review paper presents a review of the basic working, different types of available Semiconductor lasers such as DFB, ECDL, MQW etc & its comparative analysis, advantages & disadvantages and their scope in future.
